content.problemStatement:
You are given a positive integer XXX which is at most 10810^8108.

Find three **distinct** non-negative integers A,B,CA, B, CA,B,C that do not exceed 10910^9109 and satisfy the following equation:

(A∣B)&(B∣C)&(C∣A)=X(A \mid B) \mathbin{\&} (B \mid C) \mathbin{\&} (C \mid A) = X(A∣B)&(B∣C)&(C∣A)=X

Here, ∣\mid ∣ denotes the bitwise OR operator and &\mathbin{\&}& denotes the bitwise AND operator.
constraints: A, B, C must be pairwise distinct
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X (1 ≤ X ≤ 10^8).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output three distinct non‑negative integers A, B, C (0 ≤ A, B, C ≤ 10^9) separated by spaces on a single line such that (A|B)&(B|C)&(C|A)=X. Any valid triple is accepted.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each X we use A = X, B = X + 2^27, C = X + 2^28. The high bits 27 and 28 appear in exactly two of the three OR‑results, so after the final AND only the original bits of X remain, giving the required value.
X = 10^8. Adding 2^27 (134217728) and 2^28 (268435456) stays below 10^9 and yields a valid triple.
Both test cases follow the same construction; the produced triples are distinct and satisfy the equation.

solution.approach:
Observe that if we take two bit positions that are zero in X and set them only in two of the numbers, those bits will disappear after the final AND. Choose two high bits that are guaranteed to be zero for every allowed X, e.g. 2^27 and 2^28 (both > 10^8). For a given X set:
A = X
B = X + 2^27
C = X + 2^28
All three numbers are ≤ 10^9, are distinct, and satisfy (A|B)&(B|C)&(C|A)=X because the high bits appear in exactly two of the three OR‑results and are cleared by the AND, leaving only the bits of X.
The construction works in O(1) per test case.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    P = 1 << 27  # 134217728
    Q = 1 << 28  # 268435456
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        X = int(next(it))
        A = X
        B = X + P
        C = X + Q
        out.append(f"{A} {B} {C}")
    sys.stdout.write("\n".join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
